feed.title: Leandro Facchinetti
feed.subtitle: Leandro Facchinetti writes prose, software and music. He is a Ph.D. candidate in Computer Science at the Programming Languages Laboratory, at the Johns Hopkins University, advised by Dr. Scott Smith. Beyond research, his interests are writing, computer programming, music, typography, education, minimalism and veganism.

entry.title: Website Redesign
entry.summary: Published a website redesign that improves readability and load time.

entry.title: Email Migration: The Ultimate Solution to a Ridiculous Problem
entry.summary: Published an article about email migration.

entry.title: Programming-Language Theory Explained for the Working Programmer: Simple Interpreter
entry.summary: Published an article about interpreters. The discussion is driven by working code and is approachable to all programmers.

entry.title: Programming-Language Theory Explained for the Working Programmer: Principles of Programming Languages
entry.summary: Published an article about the essence of programming languages. The discussion is driven by working code and is approachable to all programmers.

entry.title: Playing the Game with PLT Redex
entry.summary: Published an article about (ab)using PLT Redex to play a game of Peg Solitaire, a gentle introduction to the tool that avoids complicated formalism.
